name: PR-CC 182 Sierra de Dios Padre Trail
name_original: Villanueva de la Sierra a Sierra de Dios Padre — circular por Las Pilas del Bardal
description: Hike the moderate 7.6-mile Villanueva de la Sierra to Sierra de Dios Padre circular trail for panoramic views and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva De La Sierra offers diverse trails through a landscape characterized by river valleys, hills, and natural water features. The region features a mix of easy paths along streams and more challenging routes ascending into the Sierra. These hiking trails around Villanueva De La Sierra provide opportunities to explore the natural beauty of the area, including waterfalls and wooded sections. Best hiking trails around Villanueva De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is PR-CC 182 Sierra de Dios Padre Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This route offers steady climbs and views of the Sierra. Another top favourite among local hikers is Chorrituelo Waterfall – Wooden Bridge Over Stream loop from Río Ovejuela, an easy 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This trail follows a river and passes a notable waterfall. Local hikers also love the Wooden Bridge Over Stream – Chorrituelo Waterfall loop from Ovejuela, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail leading through river valleys and challenging ascents, often completed in about 5 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva De La Sierra is defined by river valleys, hills, and waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family friendly walks to more challenging hikes. The routes in Villanueva De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Villanueva De La Sierra?
A: Yes, Villanueva De La Sierra offers several easy trails suitable for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinofranqueado – Pinofranqueado Bridge loop from Pinofranqueado&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.2 km path that follows a river, perfect for a relaxed outing. Overall, there are 5 easy routes available in the area.

Q: Where can I find more challenging hikes around Villanueva De La Sierra?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the region features routes with significant ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden Bridge Over Stream – Chorrituelo Waterfall loop from Ovejuela&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.6 km trail with considerable elevation gain, often taking over 5 hours to complete. This route provides a good test of endurance through river valleys and hills.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Villanueva De La Sierra?
A: Many of the trails in Villanueva De La Sierra are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinofranqueado – Pinofranqueado Bridge loop from Pinofranqueado&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19432706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrituelo Waterfall – Wooden Bridge Over Stream loop from Ovejuela&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.9 km option.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The trails in Villanueva De La Sierra are known for their natural beauty, including several water features. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrituelo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is accessible via multiple routes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the landscape.

Q: Is hiking in Villanueva De La Sierra dog-friendly?
A: Generally, the natural trails around Villanueva De La Sierra are suitable for dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet. Please check local regulations for specific areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villanueva De La Sierra?
A: The spring and autumn months are typically ideal for hiking in Villanueva De La Sierra, offering pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn showcases vibrant foliage. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable, and winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or summits to reach on the hikes?
A: Yes, hikers can enjoy scenic views from various points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive vistas. For those looking to conquer a peak, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3865817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Dios Padre&lt;/a&gt; is a notable summit in the area, offering rewarding views after a steady climb.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Villanueva De La Sierra?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Villanueva De La Sierra, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 16 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, including river valleys and natural water features, as well as the mix of easy and challenging routes that cater to different abilities.

Q: Are there facilities like picnic areas along the routes?
A: Yes, for those planning a longer day out, there are facilities available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area, Shelter, and Natural Pool El Cuadro&lt;/a&gt; is a great spot to rest, enjoy a meal, and even cool off during warmer months.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Villanueva De La Sierra?
A: There are 11 distinct hiking trails documented around Villanueva De La Sierra on komoot, offering a variety of experiences from easy riverside strolls to more demanding mountain ascents.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads might be limited, Villanueva De La Sierra itself is accessible by regional bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to the main town and then plan your hike or consider a short taxi ride to more remote starting points.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Villanueva De La Sierra?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the main villages and towns, such as Villanueva De La Sierra or Pinofranqueado, which serve as common starting points for many trails. Look for designated parking areas or roadside spots, being mindful of local regulations and private property.
tips: Start of the route in the small rural town of Villanueva de la Sierra at the foot of Mount Dios Padre, south of the Las Batuecas-Sierra de Francia Natural Park.
Hermitage and Pico de Dios Padre Hiking Route in Villanueva de la Sierra, Extremadura.
It is a lagoon with a fountain and a picnic area. It is a good place to rest.

faqs.question: How challenging is the hike from Villanueva de la Sierra to Sierra de Dios Padre?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered a moderate hike. While some initial sections are relatively flat, the ascent to the summit of Pico de Dios Padre involves a significant challenge: a steep climb of approximately 210 meters over just 1 kilometer, equating to a 20% incline. The descent also requires caution due to potential slipperiness. The total route is about 12.3 km with an elevation gain of 430 meters, typically taking around 3 hours and 46 minutes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies significantly. You'll start on relatively simple, flat paths, often alongside rivers and through olive groves. As you progress, the trail enters forested areas with dense Mediterranean scrub, cork oaks, chestnut trees, and extensive pine forests. The most challenging part is the ascent to Pico de Dios Padre, which is exposed and involves a series of zigzags and a long firebreak.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: Beyond the diverse landscapes, you'll encounter several notable features. The starting point, Villanueva de la Sierra, is historically significant for its "Fiesta del Árbol." The summit of Pico de Dios Padre offers spectacular panoramic views, a watchtower, a geodesic vertex, and a small hermitage. You'll also pass Las Pilas del Bardal, a scenic spot with a lagoon and a fountain.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Villanueva de la Sierra to Sierra de Dios Padre trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available in Villanueva de la Sierra, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked within the village, allowing easy access to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail starts in Villanueva de la Sierra, which may have some local bus services, direct public transport options specifically for hikers to the trailhead might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for Villanueva de la Sierra if you plan to rely on public transport.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn are generally ideal. These seasons offer milder temperatures, vibrant foliage, and comfortable hiking conditions. Summer can be very hot, especially on the exposed sections leading to the summit, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially slippery conditions.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, due to the varied terrain, including steep and exposed sections, ensure your dog is fit enough for the hike. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Villanueva de la Sierra to Sierra de Dios Padre circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for hiking to Sierra de Dios Padre summit?
faqs.answer: The main challenge is the steep and exposed ascent to the Pico de Dios Padre summit. This section involves a significant incline over a short distance. It's recommended to wear appropriate hiking footwear with good grip, carry plenty of water, and be prepared for sun exposure. Starting early in the day can help avoid the midday heat, especially in warmer months.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino del Bardal and the Corta Fuegos Peñas Blancas.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While some initial sections are relatively easy, the overall trail is considered moderate due to the steep and challenging ascent to Pico de Dios Padre. Beginners or families with young children might find the summit climb too demanding. However, the flatter sections around Villanueva de la Sierra and Las Pilas del Bardal could be suitable for shorter, less strenuous walks.
